story.soThat: containerized development ortamında çalışabileyim ve hot reload desteğinden yararlanabileyim

subtasks.subtask: Create `docker/` directory in project root
Create `docker/Dockerfile.dev` file
Set base image to `node:20-alpine`
Set working directory to `/app`
Copy `package*.json` files
Run `npm ci` to install dependencies
Copy Prisma schema for client generation
Run `npx prisma generate` to generate Prisma Client
Expose port 3000
Set CMD to `["npm", "run", "start:dev"]`
Document volume mount pattern in Dockerfile comments
Add instruction: Source code should be mounted to `/app`
Add instruction: `node_modules` should be excluded from mount (container's node_modules used)
Verify NestJS watch mode configured in package.json (`start:dev` script)
Document hot reload expectations (< 3s restart on file change)
Create `.dockerignore` file in project root
Add `node_modules/` to exclude
Add `dist/` to exclude
Add `coverage/` to exclude
Add `.git/` to exclude
Add `.env*` pattern to exclude (except .env.example)
Add `*.md` files to exclude (README, docs)
Add `.vscode/` and `.idea/` IDE folders to exclude
Add test directories: `test/`, `__tests__/`, `*.spec.ts` to exclude
Build Docker image: `docker build -f docker/Dockerfile.dev -t boilerplate:dev .`
Verify image build succeeds without errors
Check image size (should be optimized due to Alpine base)
Run container: `docker run -p 3000:3000 boilerplate:dev`
Verify NestJS dev server starts successfully
Verify app accessible at http://localhost:3000
Test with volume mount: `docker run -p 3000:3000 -v $(pwd):/app -v /app/node_modules boilerplate:dev`
Verify hot reload works by editing a file
Add comments to Dockerfile explaining each step
Document Dockerfile purpose: "Development-only, hot reload enabled"
Document volume mount requirements in Dockerfile header comment
Add note about .dockerignore purpose and excluded files

criterion.title: Volume Mount Support
criterion.given: hot reload için source code mount desteği eklenmiş

then.item: Source code volume mount ile container'a aktarılabiliyor
Image size optimize edilmiş (Alpine base sayesinde)
Container başlatıldığında NestJS dev server çalışıyor

document.title: Epic Technical Specification: Development Environment
document.snippet: Development container definition with node:20-alpine base, npm ci dependencies, hot reload support via volume mount, and watch mode enabled.

document.title: Epic Technical Specification: Development Environment
document.snippet: node:20-alpine chosen for minimal size (~100MB vs ~900MB Debian). Alpine uses musl libc, may require python3/make/g++ for native modules like bcrypt, puppeteer.

document.title: Epic Technical Specification: Development Environment
document.snippet: Development container startup target: < 30s first build, < 10s subsequent starts. Hot reload: < 3s restart on file change. Image size: < 500MB.

document.title: Deployment Architecture
document.snippet: Development Dockerfile pattern with volume mounts (.:/app, /app/node_modules excluded), environment variables injected, depends_on database services.

document.title: Epic 10: Development Environment
document.snippet: Docker-based local development environment for NestJS with hot reload, volume mounts, and .dockerignore optimization.

artifact.reason: NestJS watch mode command that will be used as Dockerfile CMD. Enables hot reload on file changes.
NestJS build command for TypeScript compilation. Not used in dev Dockerfile but validates project structure.
Prisma client generation command. Must be run in Dockerfile to generate client types for TypeScript.
Prisma schema file that must be copied to Docker image for prisma generate command. Required before application starts.

package.name: @nestjs/core
package.reason: NestJS framework core - required for application runtime

package.name: @nestjs/cli
package.reason: NestJS CLI for watch mode (nest start --watch) - required for hot reload

package.name: @prisma/client
package.reason: Prisma client library - generated during Docker build

package.name: prisma
package.reason: Prisma CLI for code generation - required during Docker build

package.name: typescript
package.reason: TypeScript compiler - required for watch mode compilation

package.name: ts-node
package.reason: TypeScript execution engine - required for NestJS watch mode

package.name: bcrypt
package.reason: Native module - may require Alpine build tools (python3, make, g++)

package.name: puppeteer
package.reason: Native module - may require Alpine build tools and Chromium dependencies

constraint.rule: Use node:20-alpine as base image (Architecture standard for minimal image size)
Development Dockerfile separate from Production Dockerfile (Epic 11). Dev: single-stage, root user, hot reload. Prod: multi-stage, non-root, optimized.
Layer caching optimization: Copy package.json first, run npm ci, then copy Prisma schema, then generate client. Source code NOT copied (volume mounted).
Volume mount pattern: Source code at /app, node_modules excluded (/app/node_modules). Prevents host-container binary incompatibility.
.dockerignore must exclude: node_modules, dist, coverage, .git, .env* (except .env.example). No secrets in image.
Manual testing required (no automated tests for Dockerfile). Verify: build succeeds, image size < 500MB, container starts, hot reload < 3s.

tests.standards: Manual testing is required for Dockerfile validation as there are no automated tests for Docker infrastructure. Testing follows component-level validation approach from Epic 10 test strategy. Key frameworks: Docker CLI for build/run verification, curl/browser for endpoint accessibility, file system inspection for .dockerignore validation. Test execution is developer-driven during story implementation phase.

locations.location: Validation commands in story Dev Notes section

testIdea.approach: Run `docker build -f docker/Dockerfile.dev -t boilerplate:dev .` and verify: build succeeds without errors, image size < 500MB (check with `docker images`), layers cached on second build (rebuild time < 10s)

testIdea.description: Test 2: Volume Mount and Hot Reload
testIdea.approach: Start container with volume mount, edit src/main.ts (add console.log), save file, observe logs for "File change detected", "Restarting", verify restart < 3s, check new log appears in output
Build image, run `docker run --rm boilerplate:dev ls -la /app`, verify node_modules NOT copied (will be installed), dist NOT present, coverage NOT present, .git NOT present

Run container with proper volume mounts, access http://localhost:3000, verify NestJS app responds, access http://localhost:3000/api/docs for Swagger, verify logs show "Application is running on: http://0.0.0.0:3000"
Verify bcrypt and puppeteer native modules work in Alpine container. If build fails, add Alpine build tools: `apk add --no-cache python3 make g++` to Dockerfile
